project_structure: Cocos Creator 2.x editor extension plugin with flat module architecture. The project is a dockable panel plugin ('ccc-smart-component-manager') that provides smart component search/management for the Cocos Creator editor. Core modules at root level: main.js (entry point), panel/index.js (UI panel with inline CSS/HTML/JS), search-engine.js, data-manager.js, property-mounter.js, logger.js, i18n-helper.js, scene-accessor.js (scene-script). No build system, no bundler, no npm dependencies for production - runs directly in Cocos Creator's Electron-based editor environment using CommonJS require().

relevant_files.rationale: The single monolithic UI file containing ALL panel CSS styles (inline ~600 lines of CSS variables and rules), HTML template (inline string), and JS panel logic (~3000 lines). This is the primary modify_target for any UI renovation - all visual styling, layout, and interaction code lives here.

Defines plugin metadata, panel configuration (type: dockable, width: 450, height: 120, min-height: 60), main entry point, scene-script binding, and menu registration. Panel dimensions and type constrain UI layout possibilities.

Provides DataManager singleton used by panel/index.js for favorites, usage stats, settings (autoPropertyMount, shortcuts, confirmations, logLevel). UI renovation must maintain compatibility with DataManager's getters/setters API for settings dialogs and favorite toggles.

SearchEngine singleton consumed by scene-accessor.js and indirectly drives panel search results display. Returns match objects with score/type/isFavorite fields that the panel UI renders with highlighting via highlightMatch().

Scene-script running in Cocos Creator's scene context, handles IPC messages from panel (add-component, del-component, input-query, move-component, execute-auto-mount). UI renovation must preserve all IPC message names and payload formats to maintain scene-panel communication.
Provides t() translation function used in panel/index.js for UI text labels. UI renovation should use t() for any new text strings to maintain i18n support.
Chinese translation strings for all UI labels including PANEL, ADVANCED_PANEL, SEARCH, and COMMON sections. New UI elements will need corresponding translation keys added here.
Logger singleton with two levels (ALL/ERROR_ONLY) used across all modules. Uses Editor.log() for output. UI renovation may need to update log references if panel structure changes.

Auto-docking script that programmatically docks the plugin panel next to the Inspector panel using internal Cocos Creator renderer APIs (dockUtil, panelMgr, dropMgr). Hardcoded package name 'add_component' suggests legacy origin. UI renovation must not break docking behavior.

PropertyMounter class for auto-mounting component properties. Lazy-loaded by scene-accessor.js. UI has 'execute auto mount' button that triggers this module. UI renovation needs to preserve the auto-mount trigger UI elements.

TypeScript type definitions for Cocos Creator Editor API (Editor.Panel, Editor.Ipc, Editor.Selection, Editor.Scene, Editor.UI). Documents the available API surface for panel development and UI constraints.

Runtime user data file storing favorites, usage counts, and settings (logLevel, autoPropertyMount, shortcuts, confirmations). UI settings dialogs read/write this via DataManager. Schema defines what settings UI must expose.

patterns: 1. **Monolithic panel pattern**: All UI (CSS+HTML+JS) lives in a single panel/index.js file using Editor.Panel.extend() with inline style/template strings - no external CSS files, no component framework, no templating engine. 2. **CSS Variables design system**: Panel uses CSS custom properties (--primary-color, --bg-primary, --text-primary, etc.) with a dark theme (slate/indigo palette). Tailwind-inspired naming convention. 3. **IPC communication pattern**: Panel communicates with scene-script via Editor.Scene.callSceneScript() for scene operations and Editor.Ipc.sendToPanel() for responses. Message names follow 'ccc-smart-component-manager:action' convention. 4. **Singleton module pattern**: All core modules (DataManager, SearchEngine, Logger) export singleton instances via module.exports = new Class(). 5. **Electron require pattern**: Panel uses require('electron').remote for native dialogs and clipboard. 6. **Context menu pattern**: Right-click menus built with electron.remote.Menu.buildFromTemplate(). 7. **No framework**: Pure vanilla JS DOM manipulation - createElement, querySelector, addEventListener, innerHTML assignments.
dependencies: **Runtime Dependencies (zero npm packages)**: The plugin has NO external npm dependencies for production. It relies entirely on: (1) **Cocos Creator Editor API** - Editor.Panel, Editor.Ipc, Editor.Scene, Editor.Selection, Editor.UI (provided by host environment); (2) **Cocos Creator Engine API** - cc.js, cc.Component, cc.Node, cc.Class, cc.director (provided by engine in scene context); (3) **Electron API** - require('electron').remote for native dialogs (Menu, dialog, clipboard); (4) **Node.js built-ins** - fs (file I/O for user-data.json), path (path resolution). **Internal module dependency graph**: panel/index.js -> {i18n-helper, data-manager, logger}; scene-accessor.js -> {search-engine, data-manager, logger, property-mounter}; search-engine.js -> {data-manager, logger}; data-manager.js -> {logger, fs, path}; i18n-helper.js -> {path}; property-mounter.js -> {logger}; logger.js -> (none). **Dev Dependencies**: tests/ directory has its own node_modules with Jest for testing. **UI Framework**: None - pure vanilla JavaScript with inline CSS/HTML strings.
integration_points: 1. **Editor.Panel.extend()** (panel/index.js:12) - Core panel registration API, defines style/template/messages/listeners lifecycle. 2. **Editor.Scene.callSceneScript()** (panel/index.js:1674,1719,1821,1851,2474,2484,2581,2803,2905) - All scene operations go through this IPC bridge with callback pattern. 3. **Editor.Selection.curSelection('node')** (panel/index.js:1703,1803,1831,2181,2456,2468,2534,2648,2770,2888,3026) - Node selection state query used extensively for context-aware UI. 4. **Editor.Ipc.sendToPanel()** (scene-accessor.js:109,138,168,177,271,340,350) - Scene-to-panel result callbacks. 5. **require('electron').remote** (panel/index.js:1705,1729,1766,1802,2466) - Native menus and dialogs. 6. **Editor.Panel.open/close()** (main.js:14,17) - Panel lifecycle management. 7. **template-auto-dock.js dockUtil.dropTab()** (template-auto-dock.js:60) - Internal Cocos Creator docking API for auto-positioning next to Inspector panel.
constraints: 1. **Cocos Creator 2.x Editor Extension API**: Must use Editor.Panel.extend() pattern with inline style/template strings - cannot use modern web frameworks (React/Vue/Svelte) as the panel runs in Cocos Creator's custom Electron renderer with specific panel lifecycle. 2. **No build pipeline**: No webpack/vite/rollup configured - all JS must be directly executable CommonJS modules. Adding a build step would be a significant architectural change. 3. **Electron version locked to Cocos Creator**: The Electron version is whatever Cocos Creator 2.x ships (likely Electron 2-4), limiting available Web APIs and CSS features (no CSS nesting, limited grid support, check flexbox compatibility). 4. **Panel size constraints**: package.json defines width:450, height:120, min-height:60 - the panel is designed to be compact and dockable. 5. **No external CSS/font loading**: All styles must be inline in the style string property - no external stylesheet imports, no CDN fonts. 6. **Shadow DOM isolation**: :host selector in use suggests the panel runs in Shadow DOM, isolating styles but preventing global CSS access. 7. **IPC message contract**: All message names (ccc-smart-component-manager:*) and payload formats are shared between panel and scene-script - changing them requires coordinated updates. 8. **Single-file UI architecture**: The ~3000-line panel/index.js is the only UI file - no component decomposition possible without build tooling.

clarification_needs.question: What is the target Cocos Creator version for this UI renovation?
clarification_needs.context: The plugin uses Cocos Creator 2.x Editor Extension API (Editor.Panel.extend pattern). Cocos Creator 3.x has a completely different extension system. The available CSS features, Electron version, and Editor APIs differ significantly between 2.x versions.
options: Plan migration to Cocos Creator 3.x extension system

clarification_needs.question: Should the UI renovation stay within the current single-file architecture or introduce a build pipeline?
clarification_needs.context: Currently all UI code (CSS+HTML+JS ~3000 lines) is in one panel/index.js file with inline strings. A build pipeline (webpack/rollup) would enable CSS modules, component splitting, and modern tooling but adds complexity and changes the development workflow significantly.
options: Stay single-file: improve inline CSS/HTML/JS within current architecture
Add minimal build pipeline: enable CSS extraction and JS bundling
Major restructure: introduce web component pattern with build tooling

clarification_needs.question: What is the scope of 'UI renovation' - visual refresh only or functional UI redesign?
clarification_needs.context: The current panel has extensive functionality: search with fuzzy matching, favorites, component list management, drag-to-reorder, context menus, settings dialogs, stats display, and auto-mount triggers. A visual-only refresh can reuse existing DOM structure; a functional redesign may require rethinking interaction patterns.
options: Visual refresh only: update colors, typography, spacing, animations within existing layout
Visual + interaction improvements: better search UX, improved list rendering, accessibility
Full UI redesign: rethink layout, navigation, and interaction patterns from scratch

_metadata.task_description: Plan for renovating/redesigning the current project's UI using ui-ux-pro-max design intelligence. Need to understand the dependency graph, external libraries, and technology constraints for UI renovation.
